**TERRAVOX SYSTEMS, INC.**

**(a Delaware corporation)**

These Amended and Restated Bylaws of Terravox Systems, Inc. (the \"Corporation\" or the \"Company\"), a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware, were originally adopted on March 14, 2016, and are hereby amended and restated effective as of November 3, 2021. These Bylaws are adopted pursuant to the provisions of the Delaware General Corporation Law (the \"DGCL\") and the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation of Terravox Systems, Inc. (the \"Certificate of Incorporation\").

**[ARTICLE I --- OFFICES]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 --- Registered Office**

The registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware shall be as set forth in the Certificate of Incorporation. The Corporation may also have offices at such other places, both within and outside the State of Delaware, as the Board of Directors may from time to time determine or as the business of the Corporation may require.

**Section 1.2 --- Principal Office**

The principal office of the Corporation shall be located at 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, or at such other place as the Board of Directors shall from time to time designate.

**[ARTICLE II --- STOCKHOLDERS]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Annual Meetings**

The annual meeting of stockholders for the election of directors and for the transaction of such other business as may properly come before the meeting shall be held at such date, time, and place (if any) as shall be determined by the Board of Directors and stated in the notice of the meeting. The Board may, in its sole discretion, determine that an annual meeting shall be held solely by means of remote communication in accordance with Section 211(a)(2) of the DGCL.

**Section 2.2 --- Special Meetings**

Special meetings of the stockholders may be called at any time by (a) the Board of Directors pursuant to a resolution adopted by a majority of the total number of directors then in office, (b) the Chairman of the Board, or (c) holders of at least twenty-five percent (25%) of the outstanding shares of voting stock of the Corporation entitled to vote generally in the election of directors. Special meetings may not be called by any other person or persons. Business transacted at any special meeting of stockholders shall be limited to the purposes stated in the notice of such meeting.

**Section 2.3 --- Notice of Meetings**

Written notice of each meeting of stockholders, whether annual or special, stating the date, time, place (if any), the means of remote communication (if any) by which stockholders may be deemed to be present in person and vote, and, in the case of a special meeting, the purpose or purposes for which the meeting is called, shall be given not less than ten (10) nor more than sixty (60) days before the date of the meeting to each stockholder entitled to vote at such meeting. Notice may be given by electronic transmission in accordance with Section 232 of the DGCL.

**Section 2.4 --- Quorum**

At each meeting of stockholders, except where otherwise required by law, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, the holders of a majority of the outstanding shares of stock entitled to vote at such meeting, present in person or represented by proxy, shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business. In the absence of a quorum, the holders of a majority of the shares present in person or represented by proxy and entitled to vote may adjourn the meeting from time to time without notice other than announcement at the meeting, until a quorum shall be present.

**Section 2.5 --- Voting**

Except as otherwise provided by the DGCL, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, each stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each share of capital stock held by such stockholder. Except as otherwise provided by the DGCL, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, all matters brought before a meeting of stockholders shall be decided by the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of the shares present in person or represented by proxy at the meeting and entitled to vote on the matter. Directors shall be elected by a plurality of the votes cast. Voting at meetings of stockholders need not be by written ballot unless the Board of Directors, in its discretion, or the officer of the Corporation presiding at the meeting, in such officer\'s discretion, shall so require.

**Section 2.6 --- Action by Written Consent**

Unless otherwise provided in the Certificate of Incorporation, any action required or permitted to be taken at any annual or special meeting of stockholders of the Corporation may be taken without a meeting, without prior notice, and without a vote, if a consent or consents in writing, setting forth the action so taken, shall be signed by the holders of outstanding stock having not less than the minimum number of votes that would be necessary to authorize or take such action at a meeting at which all shares entitled to vote thereon were present and voted. Prompt notice of the taking of corporate action without a meeting by less than unanimous written consent shall be given to those stockholders who have not consented in writing and who, if the action had been taken at a meeting, would have been entitled to notice of the meeting if the record date for notice of such meeting had been the date that written consents signed by a sufficient number of holders to take the action were delivered to the Corporation as provided in Section 228 of the DGCL. Every written consent shall bear the date of signature of each stockholder who signs the consent, and no written consent shall be effective to take the corporate action referred to therein unless, within sixty (60) days of the earliest dated consent delivered to the Corporation in the manner required by Section 228 of the DGCL, written consents signed by a sufficient number of stockholders to take the action are delivered to the Corporation.

**Section 2.7 --- Record Date**

In order that the Corporation may determine the stockholders entitled to notice of any meeting of stockholders or any adjournment thereof, the Board of Directors may fix a record date, which record date shall not precede the date upon which the resolution fixing the record date is adopted by the Board and shall not be more than sixty (60) nor less than ten (10) days before the date of such meeting. If the Board does not fix a record date, the record date shall be the close of business on the day next preceding the day on which notice is given, or if notice is waived, at the close of business on the day next preceding the day on which the meeting is held. For purposes of determining stockholders entitled to consent to corporate action in writing without a meeting, if no record date has been fixed by the Board, the record date shall be the first date on which a signed written consent setting forth the action taken or proposed is delivered to the Corporation in accordance with Section 228 of the DGCL. A determination of stockholders of record entitled to notice of or to vote at a meeting of stockholders shall apply to any adjournment of the meeting; *provided, however*, that the Board of Directors may fix a new record date for the adjourned meeting.

**Section 2.8 --- Proxies**

Each stockholder entitled to vote at a meeting of stockholders may authorize another person or persons to act for such stockholder by proxy. Such proxy shall be filed with the Secretary of the Corporation before or at the time of the meeting. No proxy shall be voted or acted upon after three (3) years from its date, unless the proxy provides for a longer period. A proxy shall be irrevocable if it states that it is irrevocable and if, and only as long as, it is coupled with an interest sufficient in law to support an irrevocable power.

**Section 2.9 --- Advance Notice of Stockholder Business and Nominations**

**(a) Annual Meetings.** Nominations of persons for election to the Board of Directors and the proposal of other business to be considered by the stockholders may be made at an annual meeting of stockholders only (i) pursuant to the Corporation\'s notice of meeting (or any supplement thereto), (ii) by or at the direction of the Board of Directors or any committee thereof, or (iii) by any stockholder of the Corporation who is a stockholder of record at the time the notice provided for in this Section 2.9 is delivered to the Secretary, who is entitled to vote at the meeting, and who complies with the notice procedures set forth in this Section 2.9.

**(b) Timing.** For nominations or other business to be properly brought before an annual meeting by a stockholder pursuant to clause (iii) of paragraph (a) above, the stockholder must have given timely notice thereof in writing to the Secretary of the Corporation. To be timely, a stockholder\'s notice shall be delivered to the Secretary at the principal executive offices of the Corporation not less than ninety (90) days nor more than one hundred twenty (120) days prior to the first anniversary of the preceding year\'s annual meeting; *provided, however*, that in the event the annual meeting is first convened more than thirty (30) days before or more than sixty (60) days after such anniversary date, or if no annual meeting was held in the preceding year, notice by the stockholder must be so delivered not earlier than the one hundred twentieth (120th) day prior to such annual meeting and not later than the close of business on the later of the ninetieth (90th) day prior to such annual meeting or the tenth (10th) day following the day on which public announcement of the date of such meeting is first made.

**(c) Content of Notice.** A stockholder\'s notice shall set forth: (i) as to each person whom the stockholder proposes to nominate for election as a director, all information relating to such person that is required to be disclosed in solicitations of proxies for election of directors in an election contest, or is otherwise required, in each case pursuant to and in accordance with Regulation 14A under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the \"Exchange Act\"), including such person\'s written consent to being named in the Corporation\'s proxy statement as a nominee and to serving as a director if elected; (ii) as to any other business that the stockholder proposes to bring before the meeting, a brief description of the business desired to be brought before the meeting, the text of the proposal or business (including the text of any resolutions proposed for consideration), the reasons for conducting such business at the meeting, and any material interest in such business of such stockholder and the beneficial owner, if any, on whose behalf the proposal is made; and (iii) as to the stockholder giving the notice and the beneficial owner, if any, on whose behalf the nomination or proposal is made, (A) the name and address of such stockholder, as they appear on the Corporation\'s books, and of such beneficial owner, (B) the class or series and number of shares of capital stock of the Corporation which are owned beneficially and of record by such stockholder and such beneficial owner, and (C) a representation that the stockholder intends to appear in person or by proxy at the meeting to propose such business or nomination.

**(d) Special Meetings.** Only such business as shall have been brought before a special meeting of stockholders pursuant to the Corporation\'s notice of meeting shall be conducted at such meeting. Nominations of persons for election to the Board of Directors may be made at a special meeting of stockholders at which directors are to be elected only (i) by or at the direction of the Board of Directors or (ii) by any stockholder of the Corporation who is a stockholder of record at the time the notice provided for in this Section 2.9 is delivered to the Secretary and who complies with the notice procedures set forth in this Section 2.9, provided that the stockholder\'s notice required by paragraph (b) above shall be delivered to the Secretary at the principal executive offices of the Corporation not earlier than the one hundred twentieth (120th) day prior to such special meeting and not later than the close of business on the later of the ninetieth (90th) day prior to such special meeting or the tenth (10th) day following the day on which public announcement is first made of the date of the special meeting and of the nominees proposed by the Board of Directors to be elected at such meeting.

**Section 2.10 --- Inspectors of Election**

The Corporation may, and shall if required by law, in advance of any meeting of stockholders, appoint one or more inspectors of election to act at such meeting and make a written report thereof. Such inspectors shall perform such duties as shall be specified by the Board of Directors or as required by Section 231 of the DGCL, including ascertaining the number of shares outstanding and the voting power of each, determining the shares represented at the meeting and the validity of proxies and ballots, counting all votes and ballots, and determining the result.

**[ARTICLE III --- BOARD OF DIRECTORS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- General Powers**

The business and affairs of the Corporation shall be managed by or under the direction of the Board of Directors, except as may be otherwise provided by the DGCL or the Certificate of Incorporation. Directors need not be stockholders or residents of the State of Delaware.

**Section 3.2 --- Number, Election, and Term**

The number of directors which shall constitute the whole Board of Directors shall be seven (7), or such other number as may be fixed from time to time by resolution of the Board of Directors adopted by the affirmative vote of a majority of the total number of directors then in office; *provided, however*, that any increase or decrease in the authorized number of directors shall also be subject to the provisions of the Certificate of Incorporation (including any protective provisions set forth therein) and any agreements among the stockholders of the Corporation. Each director shall hold office until such director\'s successor is duly elected and qualified, or until such director\'s earlier death, resignation, or removal. Directors shall be elected at each annual meeting of stockholders, subject to the rights of holders of any class or series of Preferred Stock to elect directors as provided in the Certificate of Incorporation.

**Section 3.3 --- Vacancies**

Unless otherwise provided in the Certificate of Incorporation, newly created directorships resulting from an increase in the number of directors or any vacancy on the Board of Directors resulting from death, resignation, removal, or other cause may be filled solely by the affirmative vote of a majority of the remaining directors then in office, even though less than a quorum. Any director elected to fill a vacancy shall hold office until the next annual meeting and until such director\'s successor shall have been duly elected and qualified.

**Section 3.4 --- Removal**

Any director may be removed, with or without cause, by the holders of a majority of the shares then entitled to vote at an election of directors, subject to the rights of holders of any class or series of Preferred Stock as provided in the Certificate of Incorporation.

**Section 3.5 --- Regular Meetings**

Regular meetings of the Board of Directors shall be held at such times and places as shall be determined from time to time by resolution of the Board of Directors. Notice of regular meetings need not be given.

**Section 3.6 --- Special Meetings**

Special meetings of the Board of Directors may be called by the Chairman of the Board, the Chief Executive Officer, or any two (2) directors. Notice of each special meeting shall be given to each director at least twenty-four (24) hours before the meeting if given by telephone, electronic transmission, or in person, or at least three (3) days before the meeting if given by mail.

**Section 3.7 --- Quorum and Voting**

A majority of the total number of directors then in office shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business at any meeting of the Board of Directors. The act of a majority of the directors present at any meeting at which a quorum is present shall be the act of the Board of Directors, unless a greater number is required by the DGCL, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws. If a quorum shall not be present at any meeting of the Board, the directors present thereat may adjourn the meeting from time to time, without notice other than announcement at the meeting, until a quorum shall be present.

**Section 3.8 --- Action by Written Consent of Directors**

Unless otherwise restricted by the Certificate of Incorporation or these Bylaws, any action required or permitted to be taken at any meeting of the Board of Directors or of any committee thereof may be taken without a meeting if all members of the Board or committee, as the case may be, consent thereto in writing or by electronic transmission, and the writing or writings or electronic transmission or transmissions are filed with the minutes of proceedings of the Board or committee. Such consent shall have the same force and effect as a unanimous vote at a meeting.

**Section 3.9 --- Meetings by Telephone or Other Communications Equipment**

Members of the Board of Directors or any committee thereof may participate in a meeting of such Board or committee by means of conference telephone or other communications equipment by means of which all persons participating in the meeting can hear each other. Participation in a meeting pursuant to this Section 3.9 shall constitute presence in person at such meeting.

**Section 3.10 --- Committees**

The Board of Directors may designate one or more committees, each committee to consist of one or more of the directors of the Corporation. The Board may designate one or more directors as alternate members of any committee, who may replace any absent or disqualified member at any committee meeting. Any such committee, to the extent provided in the resolution of the Board of Directors establishing such committee, shall have and may exercise all the powers and authority of the Board in the management of the business and affairs of the Corporation, subject to the limitations set forth in Section 141(c)(2) of the DGCL. Each committee shall keep regular minutes of its meetings and report the same to the Board when required. As of the date of adoption of these Amended and Restated Bylaws, the Board of Directors has established an Audit Committee and a Compensation Committee, each operating pursuant to a charter adopted by the Board.

**Section 3.11 --- Compensation of Directors**

Unless otherwise restricted by the Certificate of Incorporation or these Bylaws, the Board of Directors shall have the authority to fix the compensation of directors. Directors may be reimbursed for expenses of attendance at each meeting of the Board or any committee thereof. Nothing herein shall preclude any director from serving the Corporation in any other capacity and receiving compensation therefor.

**[ARTICLE IV --- OFFICERS]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Officers Generally**

The officers of the Corporation shall consist of a Chief Executive Officer, a President (which may be the same person as the Chief Executive Officer), a Secretary, and a Chief Financial Officer, and may include one or more Vice Presidents, a Treasurer, and such other officers as the Board of Directors may from time to time appoint. Any number of offices may be held by the same person. Officers shall be elected by the Board of Directors and shall serve at the pleasure of the Board.

**Section 4.2 --- Chief Executive Officer**

The Chief Executive Officer shall be the principal executive officer of the Corporation and shall, subject to the direction and control of the Board of Directors, have general supervision, direction, and control of the business and affairs of the Corporation. The Chief Executive Officer shall preside at all meetings of the stockholders and, in the absence of the Chairman of the Board, at all meetings of the Board of Directors.

**Section 4.3 --- Secretary**

The Secretary shall attend all meetings of the Board of Directors and all meetings of stockholders and record all votes and the minutes of all proceedings in a book to be kept for that purpose. The Secretary shall give, or cause to be given, notice of all meetings of the stockholders and special meetings of the Board of Directors. The Secretary shall keep in safe custody the seal of the Corporation and, when authorized by the Board, affix it to any instrument requiring it.

**Section 4.4 --- Chief Financial Officer**

The Chief Financial Officer shall keep and maintain, or cause to be kept and maintained, adequate and correct books and records of accounts of the properties and business transactions of the Corporation. The Chief Financial Officer shall deposit all money and other valuables in the name and to the credit of the Corporation with such depositaries as may be designated by the Board of Directors.

**Section 4.5 --- Removal and Resignation**

Any officer may be removed, either with or without cause, by an affirmative vote of the majority of the Board of Directors at any regular or special meeting of the Board. Any officer may resign at any time by giving written notice to the Corporation. Such resignation shall take effect at the date of receipt of such notice or at such later time as is therein specified.

**[ARTICLE V --- STOCK]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Certificates**

The shares of the Corporation may be represented by certificates or may be uncertificated as provided by the DGCL. If certificates are issued, they shall be in such form as shall be determined by the Board of Directors. Each certificate shall be signed by the Chief Executive Officer or President and the Secretary or an Assistant Secretary, and may be sealed with the seal of the Corporation or a facsimile thereof. The name and address of each stockholder, the number of shares, the class or series, and the date of issue shall be entered on the stock transfer books of the Corporation. The Corporation\'s transfer agent is Pinnacle Trust Company, located at 1201 Market Street, Suite 1600, Wilmington, DE 19801.

**Section 5.2 --- Transfers of Stock**

Transfers of stock shall be made only upon the stock transfer books of the Corporation by the holder of record thereof or by such holder\'s attorney duly authorized in writing, and, in the case of certificated shares, upon surrender of the certificate or certificates for such shares properly endorsed. The Corporation shall be entitled to treat the holder of record of any share as the holder in fact thereof and shall not be bound to recognize any equitable or other claim to or interest in such share.

**Section 5.3 --- Lost, Stolen, or Destroyed Certificates**

The Corporation may issue a new certificate or uncertificated shares in the place of any certificate theretofore issued by the Corporation alleged to have been lost, stolen, or destroyed, upon the making of an affidavit of that fact by the person claiming the certificate to be lost, stolen, or destroyed. When authorizing such issuance, the Board of Directors may, in its discretion and as a condition precedent to the issuance thereof, require the owner of such lost, stolen, or destroyed certificate to give the Corporation a bond of indemnity in such form and amount as the Board may direct as indemnity against any claim that may be made against the Corporation with respect to the certificate alleged to have been lost, stolen, or destroyed.

**[ARTICLE VI --- INDEMNIFICATION AND ADVANCEMENT OF EXPENSES]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Right to Indemnification**

The Corporation shall indemnify and hold harmless, to the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL as it presently exists or may hereafter be amended, any person who was or is made or is threatened to be made a party or is otherwise involved in any action, suit, or proceeding, whether civil, criminal, administrative, or investigative (a \"Proceeding\"), by reason of the fact that such person, or a person for whom such person is the legal representative, is or was a director or officer of the Corporation, or, while serving as a director or officer of the Corporation, is or was serving at the request of the Corporation as a director, officer, employee, or agent of another corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, or other enterprise, against all liability and loss suffered and expenses (including attorneys\' fees), judgments, fines, and amounts paid in settlement actually and reasonably incurred by such person in connection with such Proceeding. The Corporation shall be required to indemnify a person in connection with a Proceeding (or part thereof) initiated by such person only if the Proceeding (or part thereof) was authorized by the Board of Directors of the Corporation.

**Section 6.2 --- Advancement of Expenses**

The Corporation shall pay the expenses (including attorneys\' fees) incurred by a director or officer of the Corporation in defending any Proceeding in advance of its final disposition; *provided, however*, that such payment of expenses in advance of the final disposition of the Proceeding shall be made only upon receipt of an undertaking by the director or officer to repay all amounts advanced if it shall ultimately be determined by final judicial decision from which there is no further right to appeal that such director or officer is not entitled to be indemnified under this Article VI or otherwise.

**Section 6.3 --- Indemnification of Employees and Agents**

The Corporation may, to the extent authorized from time to time by the Board of Directors, provide rights to indemnification and to the advancement of expenses to employees and agents of the Corporation similar to those conferred in this Article VI to directors and officers of the Corporation.

**Section 6.4 --- Indemnification Agreements**

The Corporation may enter into individual indemnification agreements with any director, officer, employee, or agent of the Corporation providing for indemnification and advancement of expenses to the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL. The Corporation has entered into individual indemnification agreements with each of its directors.

**Section 6.5 --- Non-Exclusivity of Rights**

The rights conferred on any person by this Article VI shall not be exclusive of any other right which such person may have or hereafter acquire under any statute, provision of the Certificate of Incorporation, these Bylaws, agreement, vote of stockholders or disinterested directors, or otherwise.

**Section 6.6 --- Insurance**

The Corporation may purchase and maintain insurance on behalf of any person who is or was a director, officer, employee, or agent of the Corporation, or is or was serving at the request of the Corporation as a director, officer, employee, or agent of another corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, or other enterprise, against any liability asserted against such person and incurred by such person in any such capacity, or arising out of such person\'s status as such, whether or not the Corporation would have the power to indemnify such person against such liability under the DGCL.

**Section 6.7 --- Survival**

The rights to indemnification and advancement of expenses conferred by this Article VI shall continue as to a person who has ceased to be a director, officer, employee, or agent and shall inure to the benefit of the heirs, executors, and administrators of such person.

**[ARTICLE VII --- GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 --- Fiscal Year**

The fiscal year of the Corporation shall end on December 31 of each year, unless otherwise fixed by resolution of the Board of Directors.

**Section 7.2 --- Seal**

The Corporation may have a corporate seal, which shall be in such form as shall be approved from time to time by the Board of Directors.

**Section 7.3 --- Notices**

Whenever notice is required to be given under any provision of these Bylaws, the Certificate of Incorporation, or the DGCL, unless otherwise provided herein, such notice may be given (a) in writing and sent by mail, addressed to the person at such person\'s address as it appears on the records of the Corporation, with postage thereon prepaid, and such notice shall be deemed to be given at the time when the same shall be deposited in the United States mail, or (b) by electronic transmission in the manner provided in Section 232 of the DGCL.

**Section 7.4 --- Waiver of Notice**

Whenever any notice is required to be given under the provisions of these Bylaws, the Certificate of Incorporation, or the DGCL, a waiver thereof in writing or by electronic transmission, signed by the person or persons entitled to such notice, whether before or after the time stated therein, shall be deemed equivalent to notice. Attendance of a person at a meeting shall constitute a waiver of notice of such meeting, except when the person attends a meeting for the express purpose of objecting, at the beginning of the meeting, to the transaction of any business because the meeting is not lawfully called or convened.

**Section 7.5 --- Dividends**

Dividends upon the capital stock of the Corporation, subject to the provisions of the Certificate of Incorporation (including any preferences or restrictions applicable to Preferred Stock), if any, may be declared by the Board of Directors at any regular or special meeting. Dividends may be paid in cash, in property, or in shares of capital stock, subject to the provisions of the Certificate of Incorporation and the DGCL.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- EXCLUSIVE FORUM]{.underline}**

**Section 8.1 --- Forum for Certain Actions**

Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware (or, if the Court of Chancery does not have jurisdiction, the federal district court for the District of Delaware) shall be the sole and exclusive forum for:

> **(a)** any derivative action or proceeding brought on behalf of the Corporation;
>
> **(b)** any action asserting a claim of breach of a fiduciary duty owed by any director, officer, or other employee of the Corporation to the Corporation or the Corporation\'s stockholders;
>
> **(c)** any action asserting a claim arising pursuant to any provision of the DGCL, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws (as either may be amended from time to time); or
>
> **(d)** any action asserting a claim governed by the internal affairs doctrine.

This Section 8.1 shall not apply to claims arising under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.

**Section 8.2 --- Federal Forum for Securities Act Claims**

Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the federal district courts of the United States of America shall be the exclusive forum for the resolution of any complaint asserting a cause of action arising under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended. Any person or entity purchasing or otherwise acquiring or holding any interest in shares of capital stock of the Corporation shall be deemed to have notice of and consented to the provisions of this Article VIII.

**[ARTICLE IX --- AMENDMENTS]{.underline}**

**Section 9.1 --- Amendments by the Board**

The Board of Directors is expressly authorized to adopt, amend, alter, or repeal these Bylaws by the affirmative vote of a majority of the total number of directors then in office at any regular or special meeting of the Board, subject to the power of the stockholders to adopt, amend, alter, or repeal these Bylaws as set forth in Section 9.2 below and subject to any limitations set forth in the Certificate of Incorporation.

**Section 9.2 --- Amendments by Stockholders**

These Bylaws may also be adopted, amended, altered, or repealed by the stockholders of the Corporation; *provided, however*, that in addition to any vote of the holders of any class or series of stock of the Corporation required by law, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, the affirmative vote of the holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the voting power of all of the then-outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, voting together as a single class, shall be required to adopt, amend, alter, or repeal any provision of these Bylaws.

**[ARTICLE X --- CONSTRUCTION AND DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 10.1 --- Construction**

Unless the context requires otherwise, the general provisions, rules of construction, and definitions in the DGCL shall govern the construction of these Bylaws. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the singular number includes the plural, the plural number includes the singular, and the term \"person\" includes both an individual and an entity.

**Section 10.2 --- Certificate of Incorporation**

All references in these Bylaws to the \"Certificate of Incorporation\" shall mean the Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation, as amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, including the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on November 3, 2021.

**\\\* \\\* \\**\*

The undersigned hereby certifies that the foregoing Amended and Restated Bylaws of Terravox Systems, Inc. were duly adopted by the Board of Directors of the Corporation effective as of November 3, 2021.

TERRAVOX SYSTEMS, INC.

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Marcus Ellery

Title: Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer

Date: November 3, 2021

Originally adopted on March 14, 2016. Amended and restated on November 3, 2021.

**[STOCKHOLDERS\' AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**TERRAVOX SYSTEMS, INC.**

Dated as of November 3, 2021

This STOCKHOLDERS\' AGREEMENT (this \"**Agreement**\") is entered into as of November 3, 2021, by and among **Terravox Systems, Inc.**, a Delaware corporation (the \"**Company**\"), **Marcus Ellery** (\"**Ellery**\"), **Priya Chandrasekaran** (\"**Chandrasekaran**,\" and together with Ellery, the \"**Founders**\"), **Ridgeline Ventures Fund II, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (\"**Ridgeline**\"), **Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (\"**Northbridge**\"), and **Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (\"**Stonemill**,\" and together with Ridgeline and Northbridge, the \"**Investor Stockholders**,\" and collectively with the Founders and the Company, the \"**Parties**\").

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, the Company is a Delaware corporation (file number DE-5721438), incorporated on March 14, 2016, with its principal offices at 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723;

**WHEREAS**, the Company has entered into that certain Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, dated as of November 3, 2021 (the \"**Restated Certificate**\"), in connection with the issuance of Series C Preferred Stock;

**WHEREAS**, the Founders collectively hold 7,300,000 shares of Common Stock of the Company (Ellery: 4,200,000 shares; Chandrasekaran: 3,100,000 shares);

**WHEREAS**, Ridgeline holds 3,500,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock of the Company;

**WHEREAS**, Northbridge holds 5,200,000 shares of Series B Preferred Stock of the Company;

**WHEREAS**, Stonemill holds 7,800,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock of the Company;

**WHEREAS**, the Parties desire to set forth their respective rights and obligations with respect to the Shares (as defined below), including transfer restrictions, rights of first refusal, co-sale rights, drag-along rights, tag-along rights, and information rights; and

**WHEREAS**, the Parties are entering into this Agreement contemporaneously with that certain Voting Agreement, dated as of the date hereof, among the Company and certain of its stockholders, and that certain Registration Rights Agreement, dated as of the date hereof, among the Company and certain of its stockholders.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual promises and covenants set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties hereby agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE 1 --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 Definitions.** As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

\"**Affiliate**\" means, with respect to any Person, any entity controlling, controlled by, or under common control with such Person. For purposes of this definition, \"control\" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of voting securities representing more than fifty percent (50%) of the total voting power of such entity.

\"**Board**\" means the Board of Directors of the Company.

\"**Common Stock**\" means the Common Stock of the Company, par value \$0.001 per share.

\"**Company**\" means Terravox Systems, Inc., a Delaware corporation.

\"**Equity Securities**\" means the Common Stock, the Preferred Stock, and any securities convertible into, or exercisable or exchangeable for, Common Stock or Preferred Stock, including options, warrants, and restricted stock units.

\"**Founders**\" means Marcus Ellery and Priya Chandrasekaran.

\"**Investor Stockholders**\" means Ridgeline Ventures Fund II, LP, Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP, and Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP, and their respective successors and permitted assigns.

\"**Key Holder**\" means each Founder and any other holder of Common Stock or options to purchase Common Stock who becomes a party to this Agreement by executing a joinder agreement in the form attached hereto.

\"**Permitted Transfer**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 2.7.

\"**Person**\" means any individual, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, trust, unincorporated organization, governmental authority, or other entity.

\"**Preferred Stock**\" means, collectively, the Series A Preferred Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, the Series B Preferred Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, and the Series C Preferred Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, of the Company.

\"**Restated Certificate**\" means that certain Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation of the Company, filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on November 3, 2021, as may be amended, restated, or otherwise modified from time to time.

\"**ROFR Notice**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 2.2(a).

\"**Sale of the Company**\" means (i) any merger, consolidation, or reorganization of the Company in which the stockholders of the Company immediately prior to such transaction do not retain a majority of the voting power of the surviving or resulting entity, (ii) a sale, lease, exclusive license, or other disposition of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company, or (iii) any transaction or series of related transactions resulting in the transfer of more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding voting power of the Company.

\"**Shares**\" means all shares of capital stock of the Company now or hereafter held by any Party, including shares of Common Stock issuable upon conversion of Preferred Stock.

\"**Transfer**\" means any direct or indirect sale, assignment, transfer, pledge, hypothecation, encumbrance, or other disposition of Shares or any interest therein, whether voluntary or involuntary, by operation of law or otherwise.

**[ARTICLE 2 --- TRANSFER RESTRICTIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 General Restriction on Transfer.** No stockholder who is a party to this Agreement (each, a \"**Restricted Stockholder**\") shall Transfer any Shares except (a) in compliance with the provisions of this Article 2, (b) pursuant to a Permitted Transfer as defined in Section 2.7, or (c) in connection with a Sale of the Company consummated in accordance with Article 3. Any purported Transfer in violation of this Article 2 shall be void and of no force or effect, and the Company shall not register any such Transfer on its books or records or recognize the purported transferee as a stockholder of the Company for any purpose.

**Section 2.2 Right of First Refusal.**

> **(a) ROFR Notice.** If any Restricted Stockholder (the \"**Selling Stockholder**\") receives a bona fide written offer from a third party (other than an Affiliate of such Selling Stockholder) to purchase any Shares and desires to accept such offer, the Selling Stockholder shall, prior to consummating such Transfer, deliver written notice (the \"**ROFR Notice**\") to the Company and each other Restricted Stockholder specifying: (i) the identity of the proposed transferee, (ii) the number and class of Shares proposed to be transferred, (iii) the price per share and the form of consideration, and (iv) all other material terms and conditions of the proposed Transfer.
>
> **(b) Company\'s Right.** The Company shall have thirty (30) days from receipt of the ROFR Notice (the \"**Company ROFR Period**\") to elect to purchase all (but not less than all) of the Shares identified in the ROFR Notice at the price and on the terms set forth therein, by delivering written notice of such election to the Selling Stockholder prior to the expiration of the Company ROFR Period.
>
> **(c) Stockholder ROFR.** If the Company does not exercise its right under Section 2.2(b) within the Company ROFR Period, or exercises its right with respect to fewer than all of the Shares identified in the ROFR Notice, the Selling Stockholder shall deliver written notice to each other Restricted Stockholder (other than the Selling Stockholder), and each such Restricted Stockholder shall have twenty (20) days from receipt of such notice (the \"**Stockholder ROFR Period**\") to elect to purchase its pro rata share of the remaining Shares at the same price and on the same terms set forth in the ROFR Notice. For purposes of this Section 2.2(c), \"**pro rata share**\" means the ratio of the number of Shares (on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) held by such electing Restricted Stockholder to the total number of Shares (on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) held by all electing Restricted Stockholders (excluding the Selling Stockholder).
>
> **(d) Completion of Transfer.** If the Company and the other Restricted Stockholders do not collectively elect to purchase all of the Shares identified in the ROFR Notice, the Selling Stockholder may, subject to the co-sale right set forth in Section 2.3, transfer such Shares to the proposed transferee at a price not less than the price set forth in the ROFR Notice and on terms no more favorable to the transferee than those set forth in the ROFR Notice, provided that such Transfer is consummated within sixty (60) days after the expiration of the Stockholder ROFR Period. Any Shares not transferred within such sixty (60) day period shall again become subject to the right of first refusal provisions of this Section 2.2.

**Section 2.3 Co-Sale Right.**

> **(a)** In the event the Company and the other Restricted Stockholders do not elect to purchase all of the Shares subject to the ROFR Notice pursuant to Section 2.2, each Investor Stockholder shall have the right (the \"**Co-Sale Right**\") to participate in the proposed Transfer by selling a pro rata portion of its Shares (determined based on the ratio of Shares held by such Investor Stockholder to the total Shares held by all participating sellers, including the Selling Stockholder, each calculated on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) to the proposed transferee on the same terms and conditions as the Selling Stockholder.
>
> **(b)** Each Investor Stockholder wishing to exercise its Co-Sale Right shall deliver written notice to the Selling Stockholder within fifteen (15) days after the expiration of the Stockholder ROFR Period, specifying the number of Shares such Investor Stockholder wishes to include in the proposed Transfer.
>
> **(c)** If the proposed transferee is unwilling to purchase additional Shares from the exercising Investor Stockholders, the Selling Stockholder shall reduce the number of Shares it proposes to transfer so that each exercising Investor Stockholder may sell its full pro rata portion.
>
> **(d)** The Co-Sale Right shall not apply to any Permitted Transfer.

**Section 2.4 Tag-Along Rights.**

> **(a)** If any Founder proposes to Transfer, in a single transaction or a series of related transactions, Shares representing more than five percent (5%) of the outstanding Shares of the Company (on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) to a third party that is not an Affiliate of such Founder (a \"**Founder Transfer**\"), then each Investor Stockholder shall have the right (the \"**Tag-Along Right**\") to participate in such Founder Transfer on a pro rata basis, on the same terms and conditions as such Founder.
>
> **(b)** The Founder proposing such Transfer shall deliver written notice (the \"**Tag-Along Notice**\") to each Investor Stockholder at least twenty (20) days prior to the consummation of the Founder Transfer, specifying (i) the identity of the proposed transferee, (ii) the number and class of Shares proposed to be transferred, (iii) the price per share and the form of consideration, and (iv) all other material terms and conditions of the proposed Transfer.
>
> **(c)** Each Investor Stockholder exercising its Tag-Along Right shall deliver written notice to the transferring Founder within fifteen (15) days of receipt of the Tag-Along Notice, specifying the number of Shares such Investor Stockholder wishes to include in the Founder Transfer.
>
> **(d)** For purposes of this Section 2.4, \"**pro rata basis**\" means that each participating Investor Stockholder may include in the Founder Transfer that number of Shares equal to the product of (i) the total number of Shares to be purchased by the third-party transferee, multiplied by (ii) a fraction, the numerator of which is the total number of Shares (on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) held by such Investor Stockholder, and the denominator of which is the total number of Shares (on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) held by the transferring Founder and all participating Investor Stockholders.
>
> **(e)** If the proposed transferee is unwilling to purchase Shares from the exercising Investor Stockholders, the Founder shall not consummate the Founder Transfer unless the Founder reduces the number of Shares it proposes to transfer so that each exercising Investor Stockholder may sell its full pro rata portion.

**Section 2.5 Founder Lock-Up.** Each Founder agrees not to Transfer any Shares during the first twelve (12) months following the date of this Agreement (the \"**Lock-Up Period**\"), except for Permitted Transfers as defined in Section 2.7. Following the expiration of the Lock-Up Period, Transfers by Founders shall be subject to the right of first refusal, co-sale, and tag-along provisions of this Article 2.

**Section 2.6 Legends.** Each certificate or book-entry notation representing Shares held by a Restricted Stockholder shall bear a legend substantially in the following form:

> \"THE SHARES REPRESENTED HEREBY ARE SUBJECT TO THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF A STOCKHOLDERS\' AGREEMENT DATED AS OF NOVEMBER 3, 2021, AMONG THE COMPANY AND CERTAIN OF ITS STOCKHOLDERS, A COPY OF WHICH IS ON FILE WITH THE SECRETARY OF THE COMPANY. ANY TRANSFER OF THESE SHARES IN VIOLATION OF SUCH AGREEMENT SHALL BE VOID AND OF NO EFFECT.\"

**Section 2.7 Permitted Transfers.** Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this Article 2, the restrictions on Transfer set forth herein shall not apply to any of the following transfers (each, a \"**Permitted Transfer**\"), provided that in each case the transferee executes a joinder to this Agreement in a form reasonably acceptable to the Company, agreeing to be bound by the terms hereof:

> \(a\) Transfers by any Restricted Stockholder to an Affiliate of such Restricted Stockholder;
>
> \(b\) Transfers by a Founder to a family trust established for the benefit of such Founder or such Founder\'s spouse, children, or other lineal descendants;
>
> \(c\) Transfers upon the death of a Founder, by will or the laws of intestacy;
>
> \(d\) Distributions by an Investor Stockholder that is a partnership or limited liability company to its partners or members in accordance with the terms of its governing documents; and
>
> \(e\) Transfers in connection with a Sale of the Company consummated in accordance with Article 3.

In each case, the transferee shall agree in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as though an original party hereto.

**[ARTICLE 3 --- DRAG-ALONG RIGHTS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 Drag-Along Obligation.**

> **(a) Trigger Conditions.** If a Sale of the Company (as defined in Section 1.1) is approved by each of the following:
>
> **(i)** holders of at least a majority of the outstanding shares of Common Stock (calculated on an as-converted to Common Stock basis, treating each share of Preferred Stock as having been converted into the number of shares of Common Stock into which it is then convertible pursuant to the Restated Certificate);
>
> **(ii)** holders of at least a majority of the outstanding shares of Preferred Stock (calculated on an as-converted to Common Stock basis, voting together as a single class); **and**
>
> **(iii)** at least two (2) of the three (3) Investor Stockholders;
>
> (the satisfaction of all three conditions set forth in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) above, collectively, a \"**Drag-Along Approval**\"), then each Restricted Stockholder (including any Restricted Stockholder that did not vote in favor of such Sale of the Company) shall be required to:
>
> \(A\) vote (in person, by proxy, or by written consent) all Shares held by such Restricted Stockholder in favor of such Sale of the Company and in favor of any other matter required to be approved in connection with such Sale of the Company;
>
> \(B\) refrain from exercising any dissenters\' rights, appraisal rights, or similar rights under the Delaware General Corporation Law or any other applicable law in connection with such Sale of the Company;
>
> \(C\) take all reasonably necessary actions to consummate such Sale of the Company, including executing and delivering any agreements, certificates, instruments, or other documents required in connection therewith; and
>
> \(D\) not take any action that would impede, interfere with, or delay the consummation of such Sale of the Company.
>
> **(b) Specificity of Conditions.** For the avoidance of doubt, all three conditions set forth in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of Section 3.1(a) must be satisfied to constitute a Drag-Along Approval; the satisfaction of fewer than all three conditions shall not constitute a Drag-Along Approval and shall not trigger the obligations of this Article 3.

**Section 3.2 Terms of Sale.**

> **(a)** In any Sale of the Company effected pursuant to this Article 3, each Restricted Stockholder shall receive the same form and amount of consideration per share (on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) as every other Restricted Stockholder, or, if any Restricted Stockholder is given an option as to the form of consideration, each other Restricted Stockholder shall be given the same option; **provided, however**, that the foregoing shall not restrict the application of liquidation preferences as set forth in the Restated Certificate, and consideration shall be distributed in accordance with the liquidation waterfall provisions of the Restated Certificate as if the Sale of the Company were a Liquidation Event (as defined in the Restated Certificate).
>
> **(b)** Each Restricted Stockholder shall bear its pro rata share (based on the consideration received) of the costs and expenses of such Sale of the Company, including legal fees, financial advisory fees, and escrow costs, to the extent not otherwise paid by the Company or the acquirer.
>
> **(c)** Each Restricted Stockholder shall make customary representations and warranties, limited to title to the Shares being sold, authority and capacity to consummate the transaction, and the enforceability of the transaction documents, and shall be required to provide indemnification on a several (not joint) basis in proportion to the consideration received by such Restricted Stockholder, subject to an aggregate cap equal to such Restricted Stockholder\'s share of the total consideration.
>
> **(d)** No Restricted Stockholder shall be required to (i) enter into any non-competition, non-solicitation, or similar restrictive covenant agreement as a condition to the consummation of such Sale of the Company, or (ii) provide indemnification in excess of the total consideration received by such Restricted Stockholder (net of costs and expenses borne by such Restricted Stockholder).

**Section 3.3 Irrevocable Proxy.** Each Restricted Stockholder hereby grants to the holders constituting the Drag-Along Approval an irrevocable proxy, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, to vote such Restricted Stockholder\'s Shares in favor of a Sale of the Company for which a Drag-Along Approval has been obtained, and in favor of any matter required to be approved in connection with such Sale of the Company. This proxy is coupled with an interest and shall be valid and enforceable for the term of this Agreement. Each Restricted Stockholder hereby revokes any and all prior proxies granted with respect to such Restricted Stockholder\'s Shares that are inconsistent with the proxy granted herein.

**[ARTICLE 4 --- INFORMATION RIGHTS]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 Financial Information.**

> **(a) Annual Financial Statements.** The Company shall deliver to each Investor Stockholder holding at least 1,000,000 shares of Preferred Stock (or Common Stock issued upon conversion thereof) (each, a \"**Major Investor**\"), within one hundred twenty (120) days after the end of each fiscal year of the Company, audited consolidated financial statements of the Company for such fiscal year, including a consolidated balance sheet, statement of operations, statement of stockholders\' equity, and statement of cash flows, each prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles in the United States (\"**GAAP**\") and audited by a nationally or regionally recognized independent public accounting firm.
>
> **(b) Quarterly Financial Statements.** The Company shall deliver to each Major Investor, within forty-five (45) days after the end of each of the first three fiscal quarters of each fiscal year, unaudited consolidated financial statements of the Company for such quarter and for the year-to-date period then ended, including an unaudited consolidated balance sheet and statement of operations, each prepared in accordance with GAAP (subject to the absence of footnotes and normal year-end adjustments).
>
> **(c) Annual Budget.** The Company shall deliver to each Major Investor, at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of each fiscal year, an annual operating budget for the subsequent fiscal year, in form and substance reasonably acceptable to the Board.

For purposes of this Section 4.1, each of Ridgeline (holding 3,500,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock), Northbridge (holding 5,200,000 shares of Series B Preferred Stock), and Stonemill (holding 7,800,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock) qualifies as a Major Investor as of the date of this Agreement.

**Section 4.2 Inspection Rights.** Each Major Investor shall have the right, upon reasonable prior written notice to the Company, to inspect the books and records of the Company and to discuss the affairs, finances, and accounts of the Company with its officers, during normal business hours and in a manner that does not unreasonably interfere with the Company\'s business operations. Such inspection rights shall not be exercised more than twice per calendar year by any single Major Investor.

**Section 4.3 Confidentiality.** Each Major Investor agrees to treat all non-public information received pursuant to this Article 4 as confidential and not to disclose such information to any Person other than such Major Investor\'s directors, officers, partners, members, employees, agents, and advisors who need to know such information in connection with such Major Investor\'s investment in the Company and who are bound by obligations of confidentiality at least as protective as those set forth herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Major Investor may disclose such information (i) to the extent required by law, regulation, or legal process (provided that such Major Investor shall, to the extent legally permitted, provide the Company with prompt written notice of such requirement prior to making any such disclosure), (ii) to bona fide prospective purchasers of such Major Investor\'s Shares, subject to such prospective purchaser\'s execution of a non-disclosure agreement in a form reasonably acceptable to the Company, or (iii) in connection with the enforcement of such Major Investor\'s rights under this Agreement or any of the other agreements referenced herein.

**Section 4.4 Termination of Information Rights.** The information rights set forth in this Article 4 shall terminate upon the earliest to occur of (i) the closing of an Initial Public Offering (as defined in Section 6.1(a)), (ii) a Sale of the Company, or (iii) as to any particular Major Investor, when such Major Investor ceases to hold at least 1,000,000 shares of Preferred Stock (or Common Stock issued upon conversion thereof).

**[ARTICLE 5 --- ADDITIONAL COVENANTS]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 Proprietary Information and Inventions Agreements.** The Company shall require each officer, employee, and consultant of the Company to execute a proprietary information and inventions agreement in a form reasonably acceptable to a majority of the Investor Stockholders.

**Section 5.2 Employee Stock Option Plan.** All stock options and equity awards granted to employees, directors, or consultants of the Company shall be subject to vesting schedules approved by the Board, and all shares of Common Stock issued upon exercise of options or settlement of restricted stock units shall be subject to the Company\'s repurchase rights and the transfer restrictions of this Agreement, as applicable.

**Section 5.3 Insurance.** The Company shall maintain directors\' and officers\' liability insurance coverage in an amount not less than \$5,000,000 per occurrence, provided that such insurance is obtainable on commercially reasonable terms.

**Section 5.4 Exclusivity and Non-Solicitation.** *Reserved.*

**[ARTICLE 6 --- TERMINATION]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 Termination Events.** This Agreement shall terminate upon the earliest to occur of:

> **(a)** the closing of an **Initial Public Offering** (defined as a firm commitment underwritten public offering of the Company\'s Common Stock registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, resulting in aggregate gross proceeds to the Company of not less than \$75,000,000);
>
> **(b)** a **Sale of the Company** (as defined in Section 1.1); or
>
> **(c)** the **written consent** of the Company, the Founders, and holders of a majority of the Shares held by the Investor Stockholders (determined on an as-converted to Common Stock basis).

**Section 6.2 Survival.** Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1, (a) the provisions of Article 7 (Miscellaneous) shall survive any such termination, and (b) termination shall not relieve any Party of liability for any breach of this Agreement occurring prior to such termination.

**Section 6.3 Effect of Termination on Transfer Restrictions.** Upon the termination of this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1, all transfer restrictions, rights of first refusal, co-sale rights, tag-along rights, and drag-along rights set forth herein shall cease to be of any further force and effect, and the Company shall, upon the written request of any Restricted Stockholder, remove (or cause Pinnacle Trust Company, the Company\'s transfer agent, to remove) any legends on certificates or book-entry notations referencing this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE 7 --- MISCELLANEOUS]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 Governing Law.** This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the internal laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to principles of conflicts of laws that would cause the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

**Section 7.2 Jurisdiction and Venue.** Each Party hereby irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware (or, if the Court of Chancery declines to accept jurisdiction, the Superior Court of the State of Delaware, or the United States District Court for the District of Delaware) for the adjudication of any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, and each Party hereby irrevocably waives any objection that it may have to the laying of venue in any such court.

**Section 7.3 Entire Agreement.** This Agreement, together with the Restated Certificate, the Voting Agreement dated as of November 3, 2021, and the Registration Rights Agreement dated as of November 3, 2021, constitutes the entire agreement among the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, among the Parties with respect to such subject matter.

**Section 7.4 Amendment and Waiver.** This Agreement may be amended, and any provision hereof may be waived, only by a written instrument signed by (a) the Company, (b) the Founders, and (c) holders of a majority of the Shares held by the Investor Stockholders (determined on an as-converted to Common Stock basis). Any amendment or waiver so effected shall be binding upon all Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns. For the avoidance of doubt, any amendment to Article 3 (Drag-Along Rights) shall require the consent of each Investor Stockholder that would be adversely affected by such amendment.

**Section 7.5 Notices.** All notices, demands, requests, consents, approvals, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given (a) when delivered personally, (b) one (1) business day after being sent by nationally recognized overnight courier service, or (c) when sent by email with confirmation of receipt (excluding automated \"out of office\" or similar replies), to the addresses set forth on **Exhibit A** hereto, or to such other address as any Party may specify by written notice given in accordance with this Section 7.5.

Notices to the Company shall be addressed to:

> Terravox Systems, Inc. 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300 Austin, TX 78723
>
> Attn: Marcus Ellery, Chief Executive Officer

Notices to the Founders shall be sent to the addresses on file with the Company.

Notices to Ridgeline shall be addressed to:

> Ridgeline Ventures Fund II, LP c/o Ridgeline Ventures Management, LLC
>
> Attn: David Kleinhans

Notices to Northbridge shall be addressed to:

> Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP c/o Northbridge Growth Advisors, LLC
>
> Attn: Sandra Okafor

Notices to Stonemill shall be addressed to:

> Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP c/o Stonemill Capital GP VI, LLC
>
> Attn: Julian Trask

With a copy (which shall not constitute notice) to:

> Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP 100 Congress Avenue, Suite 2400 Austin, TX 78701
>
> Attn: Nathaniel Rourke

**Section 7.6 Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision hereof, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect. The Parties shall negotiate in good faith a substitute provision that most nearly effects the original intent of the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision.

**Section 7.7 Successors and Assigns.** This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns. No Party may assign this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Parties, except that (a) an Investor Stockholder may assign its rights hereunder to an Affiliate without the consent of the other Parties, and (b) any transferee of Shares in a Permitted Transfer who executes a joinder to this Agreement shall be deemed a Party hereto for all purposes.

**Section 7.8 Specific Performance.** Each Party acknowledges that a breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other Parties and that monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy for any such breach. Accordingly, each Party shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctions and orders of specific performance, in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bond or other security.

**Section 7.9 Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Delivery of an executed counterpart of this Agreement by electronic transmission (including by PDF or other electronic format) shall be effective as delivery of an original executed counterpart.

**Section 7.10 Aggregation of Stock.** All Shares held or acquired by Affiliated entities or persons of a Restricted Stockholder shall be aggregated together for purposes of determining the availability of any rights under this Agreement and for purposes of calculating percentage thresholds set forth herein.

**Section 7.11 No Third-Party Beneficiaries.** This Agreement is for the sole benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns, and nothing herein, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any other Person any legal or equitable right, benefit, or remedy of any nature whatsoever under or by reason of this Agreement.

**Section 7.12 Further Assurances.** Each Party shall execute and deliver such additional documents and instruments and take such further actions as may be reasonably necessary to effectuate the purposes of this Agreement.

*\[Signature Page Follows\]*

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the Parties have executed this Stockholders\' Agreement as of the date first written above.

**TERRAVOX SYSTEMS, INC.**

The following table sets forth the capitalization of the Company as of the date of this Agreement:
